stimulus: The Antikythera mechanism, an ancient Greek artifact recovered from a shipwreck in 1901, has been described as the world’s first analog computer because of its intricate gear-driven calculations of astronomical ______blank to its discovery, no comparable device of such mechanical sophistication had been documented from antiquity.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is punctuation use between sentences. The period correctly marks the boundary between the first sentence (“The...positions”) and the second sentence (“Prior...antiquity”). The adverbial phrase beginning with “Prior” modifies the second clause.

Choice A is incorrect because, without a comma preceding it, the conjunction “and” can’t be used in this way to join two main clauses. Choice C is incorrect because it results in a comma splice. Choice D is incorrect because it results in a run-on sentence, with the two clauses fused without punctuation or a conjunction.
